search_queries_used: site:endocrine.org clinical practice guideline hirsutism 2018 diagnosis biochemical testing progression menstrual disturbance
candidate_sources_rejected: polycystic-ovary-syndrome guidance as a universal hirsutism source
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Patient information was not used as professional exact evidence.
Hirsutism has multiple causes and the workflow must not infer PCOS.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Premenopausal women with hirsutism; postmenopausal, paediatric, pregnancy and isolated local-hair presentations require separate assessment.
setting_applicability: Outpatient hirsutism review by a clinician.
UAE_applicability: International specialty guidance transfers partially; UAE laboratory standards, reproductive care, prescribing, contraception and referral policy govern.
recency_verification: The official Endocrine Society 2018 guideline page and exact recommendations were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The 2018 guideline remains the current Endocrine Society hirsutism guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact diagnostic section supports documenting onset, progression, impact, associated menstrual or endocrine features, relevant negatives, focused examination and already ordered endocrine laboratory results without assigning a cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting an abnormal hirsutism score, progression, menstrual disturbance, other hyperandrogenic features, family or ethnic context and clinician-selected androgen or 17-hydroxyprogesterone testing.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact patient-important impact and reassessment section supports patient concerns, weight context and clinician-entered review or follow-up only when discussed; it does not choose therapy.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports patient-important symptom impact, preferences, treatment-response review and clinician-led reassessment. Medicine, contraception, cosmetic procedures and treatment instructions are excluded from mappings.

unresolved_source_gaps: Hair distribution or score, virilisation, menstrual pattern, pregnancy possibility, medicines, family history, exact examination, androgen values, cause, treatment and monitoring remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
